notes.content: The Mechanical Engineering Bachelor of Science is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Mechanical and Similarly Named Engineering Programs. The mechanical engineer uses basic science in the design and manufacture of complex engineering systems, requiring the application of physical and mechanical principles to the development of machines, energy conversion systems, materials, and equipment for guidance & control. Work in this broad field of engineering requires a thorough knowledge of mathematics, physics, chemistry, material science, applied mechanics, thermodynamics, heat transfer, mass transfer, electricity, manufacturing processes, and economics. The Mechanical Engineering program is designed to provide knowledge in mechanical engineering and associated applied sciences so that graduates may practice in a broad range of industries, pursue graduate studies, participate in research & development, and/or pursue entrepreneurial endeavors.
notes.contentHtml:
The Mechanical Engineering Bachelor of Science is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Mechanical and Similarly Named Engineering Programs.


The mechanical engineer uses basic science in the design and manufacture of complex engineering systems, requiring the application of physical and mechanical principles to the development of machines, energy conversion systems, materials, and equipment for guidance & control.


Work in this broad field of engineering requires a thorough knowledge of mathematics, physics, chemistry, material science, applied mechanics, thermodynamics, heat transfer, mass transfer, electricity, manufacturing processes, and economics.


The Mechanical Engineering program is designed to provide knowledge in mechanical engineering and associated applied sciences so that graduates may practice in a broad range of industries, pursue graduate studies, participate in research & development, and/or pursue entrepreneurial endeavors.

notes.content: This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students need to meet a minimum 3.10 GPA cumulative and a 3.20 GPA in major preparation coursework. All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements. Important Information: Complete the courses below: ● Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B ● Engineering (ENG) 004 and 006 and 017 and 035 ● Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D ● Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B ● Physics (PHY) 009A/B/C Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB .
notes.contentHtml:
This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students need to meet a minimum 3.10 GPA cumulative and a 3.20 GPA in major preparation coursework.


All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements.


Important Information:


Complete the courses below:

●    Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B

●    Engineering (ENG) 004 and 006 and 017 and 035

●    Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D

●    Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B

●    Physics (PHY) 009A/B/C


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: At the time of TAG submission, the College of Engineering requires a minimum 3.50 cumulative GPA and at least a 3.50 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
